title: Eni Duro
note: The debut single's video — the first time Bariga rap got a screen.

title: Omo To Shan (ft. Wizkid)
note: Two rising Lagos voices, one hook radio couldn't drop. Directed by DJ Tee.

title: "The Juice" interview with Toolz
note: On the record, denying rumors of a rival label deal — "too late for anyone to sign him."

title: Durosoke
note: Won Best Afro Hip-Hop Video at the 2013 Nigeria Music Video Awards. Directed by Clarence Peters.

title: OLIC — Olamide Live in Concert
note: OLIC 3: Beast Mode — the concert series that proved street-hop could sell out a room.

title: Wo!!
note: Shot in Bariga; sparked the #WoChallenge and pushed shaku shaku into the mainstream.

title: Infinity (ft. Omah Lay)
note: Carpe Diem's defining streamer — Clarence Peters directs the melody-first reinvention for the camera.

title: Rock
note: Lead single for UY Scuti — reggae and Afro-pop textures announcing a shorter, pop-facing chapter.

title: "People have a very limited understanding of the ghetto"
note: Print/web interview (no video embed) — on Bariga, class, and what the streets actually taught him.

title: Amapiano (Asake ft. Olamide)
note: Asake lead with Olamide's guest verse — helped carry amapiano to a global audience. Official video via AsakeVEVO.

title: Kai! (with Wizkid)
note: Fourteen years after Omo To Shan — the peer reunion that opens the self-titled chapter. Directed by Jyde Ajala.
